underwriting_details.building_type_single_family_row_house_hi: High-rise residential condominium tower with 30+ storeys, reinforced concrete construction, underground parking, and mixed owner-occupied and rental units.
underwriting_details.how_long_has_the_risk_been_vacant: Vacant since 2024-10-01 (approximately 2.5 months at time of application).
underwriting_details.use_occupancy_prior_to_vacancy: Owner-occupied residential condo unit used as the applicant’s primary residence. The unit was lived in full-time by the named insured (single occupant, no roommates) from purchase in 2018 until moving out in September 2024. Typical use included standard residential activities: remote office work from the second bedroom/den, light cooking, no home-based business, and no short-term rentals (e.g., no Airbnb or similar).
underwriting_details.reason_for_vacancy: The unit became vacant after the insured accepted a one-year work assignment in Vancouver, BC starting October 2024. Initially, Daniel planned to rent the unit out long-term but experienced several delays: (1) minor renovation work (painting, replacing bedroom flooring with vinyl plank, and updating light fixtures) scheduled for October–November 2024; (2) difficulty finding a suitable long-term tenant who could commit to a full year and meet condo board requirements; and (3) personal preference to keep the unit available in case the Vancouver assignment ends early. As a result, the condo is currently unoccupied, furnished but with valuables removed (electronics and personal documents taken to storage), and is being checked regularly by a property management company and a trusted neighbour. There is no intention to use the unit for short-term rentals while vacant; it will either remain vacant or be leased on a 12‑month lease once renovations are complete and a suitable tenant is found.
underwriting_details.who_is_responsible_for_snow_removal: Snow removal for the exterior areas, walkways, and parking entrance is handled by the condominium corporation’s contracted maintenance provider (Harbour Square Property Management Services). The insured is not responsible for any exterior snow removal. The only minor responsibility is keeping the balcony (if accumulation occurs) reasonably clear, which will be monitored by the property manager during scheduled inspections.
underwriting_details.if_the_applicant_does_not_live_within_10: The applicant is currently residing in Vancouver, BC (more than 1000 km away). Day-to-day oversight and maintenance of the condo unit are handled by: (1) Harbour Square Property Management Services, the building’s professional management company, which conducts routine building maintenance and responds to any building-wide issues; (2) a private condo management firm, Lakeshore Residential Management Inc., under a written agreement with the insured. Lakeshore Residential Management performs interior unit checks every 7–10 days, verifies that heat is functioning, tests plumbing fixtures (runs taps and flushes toilets to prevent drying of traps), inspects for leaks, and ensures windows and doors are secure. They also coordinate any repair contractors if needed; and (3) a trusted neighbour on the same floor, Michael Chen (Unit 1809), who has a spare key and has agreed to contact both the management company and the insured if he notices anything unusual (e.g., water in the hallway, noise, or signs of forced entry).
underwriting_details.describe_future_plans_for_this_property: The insured intends to keep the condo as a long-term investment property and potential future primary residence. Over the next 3–6 months, the plan is to complete the minor interior updates (fresh paint, new bedroom flooring, updated lighting, and replacement of an older dishwasher) and then list the unit for lease as a long-term unfurnished or partially furnished rental (minimum 12‑month lease) to a professional tenant who meets the condo board’s bylaws. There is no plan to convert the unit to short-term rentals or commercial use. If the Vancouver work assignment is extended beyond the original one-year term, the insured will likely continue to hold the unit as a rental property for several more years. If the assignment ends early and the insured returns to Toronto, he may move back into the condo as his primary residence. There is no current plan to sell the unit in the short term (next 1–2 years), but the insured may reassess depending on market conditions and personal circumstances. The insured is committed to maintaining the unit in good condition, keeping utilities active, and complying with all condominium rules (including quiet hours, no illicit activity, and proper waste disposal).

construction_details.construction: Reinforced concrete high-rise construction with brick and glass exterior cladding, concrete floors and ceilings between units, and fire-rated corridor walls. Standard multi-unit condominium design with central corridors and elevators.
construction_details.comments: The building is a well-maintained downtown Toronto condominium tower managed by a professional property management company. There is a fully addressable fire alarm system with smoke detectors in common areas and in each unit, as well as fire sprinklers throughout the building, including the insured unit and corridors. Fire extinguishers and hose cabinets are located on each floor near the elevator lobby. The building has 24/7 concierge/security at the main entrance, CCTV coverage in common areas and parking garage, and controlled fob access to lobby, elevators, and amenity areas. The insured unit does not have a separate in-suite burglar alarm system installed; however, the building’s controlled access and on-site security provide a high level of security. The unit’s heating and cooling are provided by a fan coil system connected to the building’s central HVAC, with the in-suite fan coil unit replaced in 2018 by the condo corporation. Electrical panel and breakers inside the unit were inspected and partially updated in 2019 when the insured had a licensed electrician replace several older breakers and install additional GFCI outlets in the kitchen and bathroom. There have been no prior insurance claims or significant losses at this unit. No history of water damage, fire, or theft in the insured’s occupancy. All renovation work currently planned is cosmetic (painting, flooring, lighting) and being performed by a licensed and insured contractor, with no structural changes or alterations to plumbing stacks or electrical risers.
